studies.title: Feasibility Study of Novel Prescription Digital Therapeutic Supporting Unobserved Buprenorphine Initiation & Adherence
studies.official_title: A Feasibility Study of PEAR-002b, a Novel Prescription Digital Therapeutic to Support Unobserved Buprenorphine Initiation and Adherence in Opioid Use Disorder Outpatients

interventions.name: PEAR-002B
interventions.description: PEAR-002B is a novel prescription digital therapeutic to support unobserved buprenorphine initiation and adherence in Opioid Use Disorder

interventions.name: reSET-O
interventions.description: reSET-O is an FDA-authorized mobile application treatment for opioid use disorder.

studies.brief_summary: The purpose of this study is to assess feasibility of an at-home digital therapy designed to support buprenorphine initiation and adherence.
studies.detailed_description:
The purpose of this study is to develop and evaluate the acceptability of a novel feature for use with the prescription digital therapeutic reSET-O.

This feature aims to improve the quality of treatment for opioid use disorder (OUD) patients starting buprenorphine initiation at home by providing new digital content and withdrawal symptom assessment to support them during this process.

This study will evaluate the acceptability of this new feature and assess success of at-home buprenorphine initiation.

_verification.reason: The study mentions reSET-O in the intervention description, but the primary focus appears to be on a different product, PEAR-002B, which is described as a novel digital therapeutic. The study is not specifically about reSET-O.
